name: John Bunch
abstract: John Bunch (December 1, 1921 – March 30, 2010) was an American jazz pianist.

about_quotes.speaker: Jay Leonhart
about_quotes.evidence: with Bucky and John — Bucky Pizzarelli and John Bunch, the group that got called New York Swing, that’s really a remarkable group to be in
about_quotes.doc_title: Jay Leonhart interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 15, 1998 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Jay Leonhart
about_quotes.evidence: with Bucky and John — Bucky Pizzarelli and John Bunch, the group that got called New York Swing, that’s really a remarkable group to be in
about_quotes.doc_title: Jay Leonhart interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, September 27, 2013 [interview 2 of 2] [transcript only]

about_quotes.speaker: Bucky Pizzarelli
about_quotes.evidence: it’s John Bunch and Jay Leonhart, just a trio... We played, I remember we played about three weeks, three or four weeks. And it was a great rhythm section.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bucky Pizzarelli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Bucky Pizzarelli
about_quotes.evidence: And then the week before we did something with John Bunch doing all Tony Bennett songs, and mostly all piano and I just played rhythm behind them.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bucky Pizzarelli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, May 23, 2003 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Wayne Andre
about_quotes.evidence: And the rhythm section was Mel Lewis, bass was Bill Crow and the piano was John Bunch.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wayne Andre interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: George Masso
about_quotes.evidence: Who else was in the Goodman Sextet at that time? ... John Bunch ...
about_quotes.doc_title: George Masso interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 10, 1997
